entry.title: Re: SAP Joule Document Grounding: What I Learned the Hard Way
entry.content:
Hi Patty,

With the destination auth type OAuth2ClientCredentials in place of OAuth2Password (This requires includePaths to be specified in the pipeline every time a new folder is created in the site) we can avoid this problem and give the Sharepoint site admin more flexibility to Add and remove users read access to particular folders in the Site. Like this Joule will be able to fetch only those docs relevant to the user and not the entire site wide access.

entry.title: Re: Restart Failed E-Signatures Without Restarting the Entire Onboarding Process post 1H 2026 Releas
entry.content:
Hello @S0027664135 - This feature is in Prod. Besides the permission you mentioned, the email template "(ONB) Onboarding Task Restart Template" also needs to be set up to notify the new hire that this task has been restarted. 

After granting the permission, from the new hire in the Onboarding Dashboard, click on 'View Details' for the 'Complete e-Signature' task. Here, there is a button to 'Restart.' After clicking on it, there is a pop up window for an optional 'Reason for restart' (I suggest to skip this as it is visible for the new hire, unless you wish them to see it). This triggers the email to the new hire to resign the documents. This restart process can be done multiple times but cannot be undone.

If you cannot see the above, I'd suggest opening a ticket with SAP Support if you have not yet. This is all working in my Prod instance, in DC68.

~Julie

entry.title: SAP on IBM i - Special considerations before upgrading the SAP Host Agent
entry.content:
In September 2025, SAP had released a security bulletin [CVE-2025-42958] for the operating system IBM i in SAP Note 3627373. It delivered patches for all supported SAP kernel releases on IBM i (7.22, 7.53, and 7.54). A month later, SAP Host Agent 7.22, patch level 69 was released. It contained code to overcome the vulnerability also for SAP systems that did not have the patches of SAP Note 3627373 applied. When the SAP Host Agent is applied in patch level 69 or higher and an SAP kernel does not have the patch level of SAP Note 3627373, there are cases when <sid>ADM user profiles are made unusable, so that the related SAP system can no longer be started.

So, before you upgrade the SAP Host Agent to the current level, make sure that your SAP systems all use the kernel patch levels that are documented in SAP Note 3627373. If you cannot apply a stack kernel (SAPEXE.SAR) in the specified level or higher, be aware that you need to apply the ILE patch for the fix, not disp+work. If you follow this rule, you are done and should not see any problems with <sid>ADM. If you want to know more about the background, you can read on.

What is the background?

Prior to security bulletin [CVE-2025-42958], all <sid>ADM user profiles were owned by group profile R3GROUP. In addition to the R3GROUP authorities that came from the ownership, additional private authorities were granted for the <sid>ADM user profile itself, so that it could be used for signing on and for database connections. Sharing the ownership of all <sid>ADM user profiles through R3GROUP was considered unsafe, so the ILE patches of SAP Note 3627373 changed the ownership of <sid>ADM to itself when the patch was applied and the SAP system was started. With that change, the private authority for <sid>ADM was no longer needed and dropped, and R3GROUP has no longer authority to <sid>ADM. So far, no issue, everything is running fine. These changes were implemented in the CRTSAPUSR tool of the kernel.

With SAP Host Agent patch level 69, code was introduced to scan the whole system for user profiles that are still owned by R3GROUP. If their naming followed the pattern <sid>ADM or <sid>OFR, the ownership was changed to itself. This code is contained in program R3SAP400/SAPINIT and executed when the SAP Host Agent is installed or upgraded, or when the system is IPLed.

With those changes in place, all <sid>ADM user profiles should be owned by itself. However, if you still have a kernel at a patch level prior to SAP Note 3627373, and if you have SAPCPE activated (see SAP Note 1632754), an old version of CRTSAPUSR may be executed by process SAPILEDX that reverts the ownership of <sid>ADM from itself to R3GROUP. During this process, the private authority for <sid>ADM is not granted, so that further attempts to use the <sid>ADM are failing.

If you experience that problem and cannot upgrade to the required ILE patch level immediately, you can solve the problem temporarily by changing the ownership of <sid>ADM back to itself. Unfortunately, each time the kernel library SAP<sid>IND is touched (e.g. during a backup), SAPILEDX will be executed and the ownership would be changed back to the incorrect value R3GROUP. To prevent this from happening, you can create a data area named SUPPORT in the kernel library as described in SAP Note 1637588. The data area should be deleted automatically when you apply the required ILE patch through the APYSIDKRN tool, but to be on the safe side, you should verify this and delete the data area manually if required.

This is the command to change the owner back to <sid>ADM:

CHGOBJOWN OBJ(<sid>ADM) OBJTYPE(*USRPRF) NEWOWN(<sid>ADM) CUROWNAUT(*REVOKE)

This is the command to create the data area SUPPORT in order to avoid changing the ownership back by SAPILEDX:

CRTDTAARA DTAARA(SAP<sid>IND/SUPPORT) TYPE(*CHAR) LEN(1)

Please note that the above is just a workaround. The recommended solution is to apply the ILE patches of SAP Note 3627373 for all affected SAP systems.

entry.title: SAP TM + HSS Integration: Implementing Matrix Routing v2
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

If you are working with SAP Transportation Management (TM) and SAP HANA Spatial Services (HSS), you’ve likely seen the recent news regarding the Matrix Routing API. According to SAP Note 3648907, the Matrix Routing v1 API has reached its end-of-life (EOL) and must be migrated to v2 before September 2026.

Additionally, this migration includes a change in the licensing provider within the OEM model: the service is shifting from HERE to TomTom.

 

To migrate from Matrix Routing v1 API to Matrix Routing v2 API, you need to implement sap note 3695260. Follow regular steps you would on implementing any SAP Note, like checking pre-requisites.

 

Please be aware that evaluating your licensing models and parameters adjustments are be necessaire steps for this migration. Notes 3648907 and 3648910 can help you on that.

 

More information about HSS and new licensing model and parameters can be found in

Frequently Asked Questions for SAP HANA Spatial Services | SAP Help Portal

SAP HANA Spatial Services Reference  (link to 2025-10-30 version)

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign Collection Management Attributes via...
entry.content: Hi @Ian2345, You can create a job with the Created On filter set to greater than or equal to today's date (for example, 30 June 2026). The date will need to be hardcoded, as there is currently no dynamic date functionality available. This approach should at least eliminate the need to create a new job every day. You would only need to update the date when necessary.

entry.title: German B2B E-Invoicing Mandate - Current State for SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

we are preparing for the German B2B E-Invoicing mandate (XRechnung / ZUGFeRD 2.1.1+, EN 16931) effective January 1, 2027 and are running SAP Business ByDesign. Before committing to a third-party add-on, we have three open questions:

1. Is a native standard solution planned?
We couldn't find any official SAP roadmap entry confirming a fully compliant out-of-the-box solution for ByDesign. Is there an official statement from SAP Product Management on this?

2. XSLT approach – does it work in production?
We found these two blog posts describing the XSLT transformation approach:

  • E-Invoicing with SAP Business ByDesign
  • eInvoice Templates for SAP Business ByDesign

Has anyone used these templates successfully in a live German B2B environment for outgoing invoices? Are there production-ready templates for XRechnung 3.0  available, and how is ongoing maintenance handled?

3. What is your current approach?
Native XSLT, third-party add-on, or waiting for SAP? Any experience is welcome!

Thanks in advance ,

Mike

entry.title: Logic to add attachments in SAP TM from code with Custom PPF action configuration.
entry.content:
For this requirement I created an adobe form in SFP.
Printed it in runtime and then added it to the Freight Order using the ROOT key in Schedule Action of:
BADI-EVAL_SCHEDCOND_PPF
Implementation method-IF_EX_EVAL_SCHEDCOND_PPF~EVALUATE_SCHEDULE_CONDITION
Below is the code snippet I used.

    DATA: lv_fm_name      TYPE rs38l_fnam,
          ls_outputparams TYPE sfpoutputparams,
          ls_docparams    TYPE sfpdocparams,
          ls_result       TYPE sfpjoboutput,
          ls_formoutput   TYPE fpformoutput.

* =====================================================================
* Create the Adobe Form
* =====================================================================
        ls_outputparams-nodialog = abap_true.
        ls_outputparams-getpdf   = abap_true.

        ls_docparams-langu   = sy-langu.

        CALL FUNCTION 'FP_JOB_OPEN'
          CHANGING
            ie_outputparams = ls_outputparams
          EXCEPTIONS
            cancel          = 1
            usage_error     = 2
            system_error    = 3
            internal_error  = 4
            OTHERS          = 5.
        IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
          TRY.
              CALL FUNCTION 'FP_FUNCTION_MODULE_NAME'
                EXPORTING
                  i_name     = lc_formname
                IMPORTING
                  e_funcname = lv_fm_name.
            CATCH cx_fp_api_repository cx_fp_api_usage cx_fp_api_internal.
          ENDTRY.

          CALL FUNCTION lv_fm_name
            EXPORTING
              /1bcdwb/docparams  = ls_docparams
            IMPORTING
              /1bcdwb/formoutput = ls_formoutput
            EXCEPTIONS
              usage_error        = 1
              system_error       = 2
              internal_error     = 3
              OTHERS             = 4.
          IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
            CALL FUNCTION 'FP_JOB_CLOSE'
              IMPORTING
                e_result       = ls_result
              EXCEPTIONS
                usage_error    = 1
                system_error   = 2
                internal_error = 3
                OTHERS         = 4.

            IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL AND ls_formoutput-pdf IS NOT INITIAL.
              DATA(lv_pdf_xstring) = ls_formoutput-pdf.
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

IF lv_pdf_xstring IS NOT INITIAL.
  CONSTANTS: lc_mimetype TYPE /bobf/s_atf_a_create_file-mime_code VALUE 'application/pdf',
             lc_atcmt    TYPE /bobf/s_atf_a_create_file-attachment_type VALUE 'ATCMT',
             lc_default  TYPE /bobf/s_atf_a_create_file-attachment_schema VALUE 'DEFAULT'.

  DATA: lt_key        TYPE /bobf/t_frw_key,
        ls_parameters TYPE /bobf/s_atf_a_create_file,
        lr_action_par TYPE REF TO /bobf/s_atf_a_create_file,
        lr_atf_root   TYPE REF TO /bobf/s_atf_root_k,
        lt_mod        TYPE /bobf/t_frw_modification,
        ls_mod        TYPE /bobf/s_frw_modification.

  APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_key ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_key>).
  <lfs_key>-key = iv_root_key.

*--Invoke Service Manger & Transaction Manager.
  TRY.
      DATA(lo_srv_tor) = /bobf/cl_tra_serv_mgr_factory=>get_service_manager( /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_bo_key ).
      DATA(lo_tra_tor) = /bobf/cl_tra_trans_mgr_factory=>get_transaction_manager( ).
    CATCH /bobf/cx_frw_contrct_violation.
      RETURN.
    CATCH cx_root.
      RETURN.
  ENDTRY.

*--Fetch the ATTACHMENTFOLDER key.
  TRY.
      lo_srv_tor->retrieve_by_association(
        EXPORTING
          iv_node_key    = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root
          it_key         = lt_key
          iv_association = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_association-root-attachmentfolder
          iv_edit_mode   = /bobf/if_conf_c=>sc_edit_read_only
        IMPORTING
          et_target_key  = DATA(lt_atf_key)
          et_failed_key  = DATA(lt_failed_key) ).
    CATCH /bobf/cx_frw_contrct_violation.
      RETURN.
    CATCH cx_root.
      RETURN.
  ENDTRY.

*--If the ATTACHMENTFOLDER is blank and there is NO Attachment attached to it.
*--Create the Folder First.
  IF lt_atf_key IS INITIAL.

    TRY.
        CREATE DATA lr_atf_root.
        lr_atf_root->key        = lo_srv_tor->get_new_key( ).
        lr_atf_root->parent_key = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root.

        ls_mod-change_mode = /bobf/if_frw_c=>sc_modify_create.
        ls_mod-association = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_association-root-attachmentfolder.
        ls_mod-source_node = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root.
        ls_mod-node        = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-attachmentfolder.
        ls_mod-source_key  = iv_root_key.
        ls_mod-key         = lr_atf_root->key.
        ls_mod-data        = lr_atf_root.
        INSERT ls_mod INTO TABLE lt_mod.

        lo_srv_tor->modify(
          EXPORTING
            it_modification = lt_mod
          IMPORTING
            eo_change       = DATA(lo_change)
            eo_message      = DATA(lo_message) ).

        lo_tra_tor->save(
          IMPORTING
            ev_rejected         = DATA(lv_rejected)
            eo_change           = lo_change
            eo_message          = lo_message ).

        INSERT VALUE #( key = lr_atf_root->key ) INTO TABLE lt_atf_key.

      CATCH /bobf/cx_frw_contrct_violation.
        RETURN.
      CATCH cx_root.
        RETURN.
    ENDTRY.

  ENDIF.

*--Populate the Attachment Details.
  ls_parameters-file_name                 = 'Hazardous Material Information.pdf'(001).
  ls_parameters-alternative_name          = 'Hazardous Material Information.pdf'(001).
  ls_parameters-name                      = 'Hazardous Material Information.pdf'(001).
  ls_parameters-content                   = iv_pdf_data.
  ls_parameters-mime_code                 = lc_mimetype.       "'application/pdf'.
  ls_parameters-description_language_code = sy-langu.
  ls_parameters-description               = 'Hazardous Material Information.pdf'(001).
  ls_parameters-description_content       = 'Hazardous Material Information.pdf'(001).
  ls_parameters-visibility_type           = abap_true.
  ls_parameters-attachment_type           = 'ATCMT'.
  ls_parameters-attachment_schema         = 'DEFAULT'.

  GET REFERENCE OF ls_parameters INTO lr_action_par.

  TRY.
      /scmtms/cl_common_helper=>get_do_keys_4_action(
        EXPORTING
          iv_host_bo_key      = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_bo_key
          iv_host_do_node_key = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-attachmentfolder
          iv_do_action_key    = /bobf/if_attachment_folder_c=>sc_action-root-create_file
        IMPORTING
          ev_action_key       = DATA(lv_action_key) ).
    CATCH /bobf/cx_frw_contrct_violation.
      RETURN.
    CATCH cx_root.
      RETURN.
  ENDTRY.

  IF lv_action_key IS INITIAL.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  TRY.
      lo_srv_tor->do_action(
        EXPORTING
          iv_act_key    = lv_action_key
          it_key        = lt_atf_key
          is_parameters = lr_action_par
        IMPORTING
          et_failed_key = DATA(lt_failed_key_atf)
          eo_change     = lo_change
          eo_message    = lo_message ).

      lo_tra_tor->save(
        IMPORTING
          ev_rejected         = lv_rejected
          eo_change           = lo_change
          eo_message          = lo_message ).

    CATCH /bobf/cx_frw_contrct_violation.
      RETURN.
    CATCH cx_root.
      RETURN.
  ENDTRY.
ENDIF.

 Note:
I had added the create attachment logic in a RFC Function Module and called it from PPF-Schedule Action BADI Implementation method STARTING NEW TASK.
As, the BADI implementation won't allow explicit SAVE.

Custom PPF Configuration Steps:

1. Run Transaction: SPPFCADM.

2. Define Action Profile and Actions

3. Custom Action.

4. Set Processing Types.

5. Condition of Configuration.

6. Technical Names->Schedule Condition.

7. Schedule Condition.

8. Schedule Condition Value: Z_US_TM_SCH_PPF_HAZARD_FORM.
Is a BADI Implementation Filter Value given for that BADI Implementation.

9. Similarly for Start Condition as well.

Inside the Badi Impl we set the flags to 4 if our conditions not met.

10. Maintain Entry in Schedule Condition.

11. Create a child class of Standard class: /SCMTMS/CL_PPF_SERV_TOR_B2B

Redefine your required methods.

METHOD method_call_by_abap.
*--------------------------------------------------------------------------
*--Types Declaration
*--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    TYPES: BEGIN OF lty_docref_data,
             btd_tco TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_docref_k-btd_tco,
             btd_id  TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_docref_k-btd_id,
           END OF lty_docref_data,

           BEGIN OF lty_stop_data,
             stop_seq_pos    TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_stop_k-stop_seq_pos,
             plan_trans_time TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_stop_k-plan_trans_time,
           END OF lty_stop_data,

           BEGIN OF lty_tor_item,
             item_cat         TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_item_tr_k-item_cat,
             main_cargo_item  TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_item_tr_k-main_cargo_item,
             loading_point_id TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_item_tr_k-loading_point_id,
           END OF lty_tor_item,

           BEGIN OF lty_soldtoparty,
             party_rco TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_party_k-party_rco,
             party_id  TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_party_k-party_id,
           END OF lty_soldtoparty,

           BEGIN OF lty_atf_doc,
             attachment_type       TYPE /bobf/s_atf_document_k-attachment_type,
             external_link_web_uri TYPE /bobf/s_atf_document_k-external_link_web_uri,
           END OF lty_atf_doc.

*--------------------------------------------------------------------------
*--Data Declaration & Constants
*--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    CONSTANTS: lc_pdf           TYPE soodk-objtp                            VALUE 'PDF',
               lc_type          TYPE w3conttype                             VALUE 'image/bmp',
               lc_set           TYPE i_setleaf-setid                        VALUE 'EMAIL_TO_CUSTOMER',
               lc_73            TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_docref_k-btd_tco         VALUE '73',
               lc_114           TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_docref_k-btd_tco         VALUE '114',
               lc_prd           TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_item_tr_k-item_cat       VALUE 'PRD',
               lc_sp            TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_party_k-party_rco        VALUE 'AG',
               lc_us_tm004      TYPE zi_tvarvctableforcusto-changeid        VALUE 'US_TM004',
               lc_04            TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_root_k-confirmation      VALUE '04',
               lc_05            TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_root_k-confirmation      VALUE '05',
               lc_l             TYPE /scmtms/s_tor_stop_k-stop_seq_pos      VALUE 'L',
               lc_comp1          TYPE zi_us_tm_setleaf-setrangefromvalue     VALUE '500001',
               lc_comp2          TYPE zi_us_tm_setleaf-setrangefromvalue     VALUE '500002',
               lc_email_trigger TYPE /bofu/s_ppf_act-ppf_action             VALUE 'ZTM_TOR_TRACKNO_NTF',
               lc_hazard_form   TYPE /bofu/s_ppf_act-ppf_action             VALUE 'ZTM_TOR_HAZARD_FORM',
               lc_name1       TYPE zi_tvarvctableforcusto-name            VALUE 'Z_EMAIL1',
               lc_name2       TYPE zi_tvarvctableforcusto-name            VALUE 'Z_EMAIL2',
               lc_mimetype      TYPE /bobf/s_atf_file_content_k-mime_code   VALUE 'application/pdf',
               lc_link          TYPE /bobf/s_atf_document_k-attachment_type VALUE 'LINK'.

    CONSTANTS: lc_company1 TYPE tdobname   VALUE 'ZCOMPANY1_LOGO',
               lc_company2 TYPE tdobname   VALUE 'ZCOMPANY2_LOGO',
               lc_graphics  TYPE tdobjectgr VALUE 'GRAPHICS',
               lc_bmap      TYPE tdidgr     VALUE 'BMAP',
               lc_bcol      TYPE tdbtype    VALUE 'BCOL'.

    CONSTANTS: lc_obj    TYPE cl_bali_header_setter=>ty_object      VALUE 'ZTM_FO_LOG',
               lc_subobj TYPE cl_bali_header_setter=>ty_subobject   VALUE 'ZTM_FO_PPF_LOG',
               lc_ext_id TYPE cl_bali_header_setter=>ty_external_id VALUE 'ZTM_FO_PPF_ACTION_LOG'.

    DATA: lt_docref_data_temp TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF lty_docref_data,
          lt_stop_data_temp   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF lty_stop_data,
          lt_tor_item_temp    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF lty_tor_item,
          lt_soldtoparty_temp TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF lty_soldtoparty,
          lt_atf_doc_temp     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF lty_atf_doc.

    DATA: lv_html      TYPE string,
          lv_html_item TYPE string.

    DATA: lt_attachment_key  TYPE /bobf/t_frw_key,
          lt_key             TYPE /bobf/t_frw_key,
          lt_stop_data       TYPE /scmtms/t_tor_stop_k,
          lt_tor_root_before TYPE /scmtms/t_tor_root_k,
          lt_tor_root_after  TYPE /scmtms/t_tor_root_k,
          lt_soldtoparty     TYPE /scmtms/t_tor_party_k,
          lt_docref_data     TYPE /scmtms/t_tor_docref_k,
          lt_tor_item        TYPE /scmtms/t_tor_item_tr_k,
          lv_sales_order     TYPE i_salesdocument-salesdocument,
          lv_delv_doc        TYPE i_deliverydocument-deliverydocument,
          lv_ship_date       TYPE string,
          lv_temp_date       TYPE string,
          ls_addrs_sel       TYPE addr1_sel,
          lv_qty             TYPE p DECIMALS 2.

    DATA: lv_hama_logo    TYPE xstring,
          lv_ener_logo    TYPE xstring,
          lv_company_logo TYPE string,
          lv_root_key     TYPE /bobf/conf_key,
          lv_read_key     TYPE char25,
          lo_container    TYPE REF TO /bofu/cl_ppf_container,
          lo_message      TYPE REF TO /bobf/if_frw_message,
          lr_name         TYPE RANGE OF zi_tvarvctableforcusto-name,
          lr_doc_status   TYPE RANGE OF /scmtms/s_tor_root_k-confirmation.

    DATA: lv_attach_size TYPE sood-objlen,
          lv_file_name   TYPE sood-objdes,
          ls_html_body   TYPE soli,
          ls_logo_solix  TYPE solix,
          lt_binary_tab  TYPE solix_tab,
          lt_html_body   TYPE soli_tab.

    DATA: lo_appl_log TYPE REF TO if_bali_log,
          lv_msg(200) TYPE c.

*--Log Creation Logic.
    TRY.
        lo_appl_log = cl_bali_log=>create_with_header(
                        header = cl_bali_header_setter=>create(
                                   object      = lc_obj
                                   subobject   = lc_subobj
                                   external_id = lc_ext_id ) ).
      CATCH cx_bali_runtime.
    ENDTRY.

*--MACRO for Log Messages.
    DEFINE log_free_text.
      IF lo_appl_log IS BOUND.
        TRY.
            lo_appl_log->add_item(
              item = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create(
                       severity = &1
                       text     = &2 ) ).
          CATCH cx_bali_runtime.
        ENDTRY.
      ENDIF.
    END-OF-DEFINITION.

*--MACRO for saving the log and returning.
    DEFINE save_log_and_return.
      IF lo_appl_log IS BOUND.
        TRY.
            cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log = lo_appl_log ).
          CATCH cx_bali_runtime.
        ENDTRY.
      ENDIF.
      RETURN.
    END-OF-DEFINITION.

*--Get the BO Root Key.
    TRY.
        IF io_container IS BOUND.
          lo_container ?= io_container.
          lv_root_key = lo_container->get_bo_root_key( ).
        ENDIF.
      CATCH: cx_sy_move_cast_error cx_sy_ref_is_initial cx_root.
    ENDTRY.

    APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_key ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_key>).
    <lfs_key>-key = lv_root_key.

*--Service Manager Initiation.
    DATA(lo_srv_mgr) = /bobf/cl_tra_serv_mgr_factory=>get_service_manager( /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_bo_key ).

    CASE is_ppf_act-ppf_action.

      WHEN lc_email_trigger.   "ZTM_TOR_TRACKNO_NTF

*--------------------------------------------------------------------------
*--Validation Check Start
*--------------------------------------------------------------------------
        TRY.
*--Only If Tracking URL, Tracking ID & Confirmation Status is 04/05.
*--Then only trigger the email to customer.
*--Fetch the Status of the Document before Status Change.
            lo_srv_mgr->retrieve(
              EXPORTING
                iv_node_key     = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root
                it_key          = lt_key
                iv_before_image = abap_true
                iv_fill_data    = abap_true
              IMPORTING
                et_data         = lt_tor_root_before
                eo_message      = lo_message ).

            IF lt_tor_root_before IS NOT INITIAL.
              DATA(lv_doc_status_before) = VALUE #( lt_tor_root_before[ 1 ]-confirmation OPTIONAL ).
            ENDIF.

*--Fetch the Status of the Document after Status Change.
            lo_srv_mgr->retrieve(
              EXPORTING
                iv_node_key  = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root
                it_key       = lt_key
                iv_fill_data = abap_true
              IMPORTING
                et_data      = lt_tor_root_after
                eo_message   = lo_message ).

            IF lt_tor_root_after IS NOT INITIAL.

              DATA(lv_doc_status_after) = VALUE #( lt_tor_root_after[ 1 ]-confirmation     OPTIONAL ).
              DATA(lv_tracking_no)      = VALUE #( lt_tor_root_after[ 1 ]-partner_mbl_id   OPTIONAL ).
              DATA(lv_carrier)          = VALUE #( lt_tor_root_after[ 1 ]-tspid            OPTIONAL ).
              DATA(lv_terms)            = VALUE #( lt_tor_root_after[ 1 ]-transsrvlvl_code OPTIONAL ).
              DATA(lv_consignee_id)     = VALUE #( lt_tor_root_after[ 1 ]-consigneeid      OPTIONAL ).
              DATA(lv_purch_orgid)      = VALUE #( lt_tor_root_after[ 1 ]-purch_org_id     OPTIONAL ).

              IF lv_purch_orgid IS NOT INITIAL.
                SELECT SINGLE setrangefromvalue
                FROM zi_us_tm_setleaf
                WHERE setid           = @lc_set         "'EMAIL_TO_CUSTOMER'
                AND setrangefromvalue = @lv_purch_orgid
                INTO @DATA(lv_purch_org).

                IF sy-subrc IS NOT INITIAL.
                  lv_msg = |Purch Org: { lv_purch_orgid } not maintained in Table: ZI_US_TM_SETLEAF| &&
                           |Set-Id: EMAIL_TO_CUSTOMER.|.
                  log_free_text if_bali_constants=>c_severity_status lv_msg.
                  rp_status = 2.
                  save_log_and_return.
                ENDIF.

              ENDIF.
            ENDIF.
          CATCH /bobf/cx_frw_contrct_violation.
        ENDTRY.

*--Fetch ATTACHMENT Folder Details.
        TRY.
            lo_srv_mgr->retrieve_by_association(
              EXPORTING
                iv_node_key    = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root
                it_key         = lt_key
                iv_association = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_association-root-attachmentfolder
              IMPORTING
                et_key_link   = DATA(lt_key_link)
                et_target_key = DATA(lt_target_key)
                et_failed_key = DATA(lt_failed_key) ).

            IF lt_key_link IS NOT INITIAL.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_attachment_key ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_attachment_key>).
              <lfs_attachment_key>-key = lt_key_link[ 1 ]-source_key.
            ENDIF.
          CATCH /bobf/cx_frw_contrct_violation.
        ENDTRY.

*--Fetch URL & Attachment PDF Data.
        TRY.
            CALL METHOD /scmtms/cl_atf_helper=>get_atf_data
              EXPORTING
                iv_bo_key       = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_bo_key
                iv_source_node  = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root
                iv_node         = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-attachmentfolder
                iv_association  = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_association-root-attachmentfolder
                it_key          = lt_attachment_key
              IMPORTING
                et_root         = DATA(lt_atf_root)
                et_document     = DATA(lt_atf_doc)
                et_file_content = DATA(lt_atf_file).
          CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(ls_exception).
        ENDTRY.

*--Tracking URL
        IF lt_atf_doc IS NOT INITIAL.
          lt_atf_doc_temp = CORRESPONDING #( lt_atf_doc ).
          SORT lt_atf_doc_temp BY attachment_type.

          READ TABLE lt_atf_doc_temp WITH KEY attachment_type = lc_link
          ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_atf_doc_temp>) BINARY SEARCH.
          IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
            DATA(lv_tracking_url) = <lfs_atf_doc_temp>-external_link_web_uri.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

        APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lr_doc_status ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_doc_status>).
        <lfs_doc_status>-sign   = cl_abap_range=>sign-including.
        <lfs_doc_status>-option = cl_abap_range=>option-equal.
        <lfs_doc_status>-low    = lc_04.

        APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lr_doc_status ASSIGNING <lfs_doc_status>.
        <lfs_doc_status>-sign   = cl_abap_range=>sign-including.
        <lfs_doc_status>-option = cl_abap_range=>option-equal.
        <lfs_doc_status>-low    = lc_05.

*--If Document Status is Unchanged RETURN the Control.
        IF lv_doc_status_before = lv_doc_status_after.
          lv_msg = |Document Status Unchanged - Return|.
          log_free_text if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error lv_msg.
          rp_status = 2.
          save_log_and_return.
        ENDIF.

*--If Document Status is not Confirmed/Send to Confirmation
        IF lv_doc_status_after NOT IN lr_doc_status.
          lv_msg = |Document Status is not Confirmed/Send to Confirmation - Return|.
          log_free_text if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error lv_msg.
          rp_status = 2.
          save_log_and_return.
        ENDIF.

*--Trigger the Enhancement only If tracking number and tracking url is maintained.
        IF lv_tracking_no IS INITIAL.
          lv_msg = |Freight Order Tracking Number not found - Return|.
          log_free_text if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error lv_msg.
          rp_status = 2.
          save_log_and_return.
        ENDIF.

        IF lv_tracking_url IS INITIAL.
          lv_msg = |Freight Order Tracking URL not found - Return|.
          log_free_text if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error lv_msg.
          rp_status = 2.
          save_log_and_return.
        ENDIF.

*--------------------------------------------------------------------------
*--Validation Check End.
*--------------------------------------------------------------------------
        TRY.
*--Fetch Sales Order & Delivery Number Document
            lo_srv_mgr->retrieve_by_association(
              EXPORTING
                iv_node_key    = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root
                it_key         = lt_key
                iv_association = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_association-root-docreference
                iv_fill_data   = abap_true
                iv_edit_mode   = /bobf/if_conf_c=>sc_edit_read_only
              IMPORTING
                et_data        = lt_docref_data
                et_target_key  = DATA(lt_target_keys)
                et_key_link    = DATA(lt_key_links)
                eo_message     = lo_message ).

            IF lt_docref_data IS NOT INITIAL.
              lt_docref_data_temp = CORRESPONDING #( lt_docref_data ).
              SORT lt_docref_data_temp BY btd_tco.
              READ TABLE lt_docref_data_temp WITH KEY btd_tco = lc_114 INTO DATA(ls_docref) BINARY SEARCH.
              IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
                DATA(lv_so_no) = ls_docref-btd_id.
              ENDIF.

              READ TABLE lt_docref_data_temp WITH KEY btd_tco = lc_73 INTO ls_docref BINARY SEARCH.
              IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
                DATA(lv_delv_no) = ls_docref-btd_id.
              ENDIF.

*--Fetch Delivery Document Details
              IF lv_delv_no IS NOT INITIAL.
                lv_delv_doc = lv_delv_no+25(10).
                SELECT SINGLE a~deliverydocument,a~documentdate,a~shippingcondition,
                              b~materialbycustomer,b~actualdeliveryquantity,
                              c~shippingconditionname
                FROM i_deliverydocument AS a
                INNER JOIN i_deliverydocumentitem AS b
                ON b~deliverydocument = b~deliverydocument
                INNER JOIN i_shippingconditiontext AS c
                ON c~shippingcondition = a~shippingcondition
                WHERE a~deliverydocument = @lv_delv_doc
                INTO @DATA(ls_delv_det).
                IF sy-subrc IS NOT INITIAL.
                  CLEAR: ls_delv_det.
                ENDIF.
              ENDIF.

*--Fetch Purchase Order Number.
              IF lv_so_no IS NOT INITIAL.
                lv_sales_order = lv_so_no+25(10).

                SELECT SINGLE purchaseorderbycustomer
                FROM i_salesdocument
                WHERE salesdocument = @lv_sales_order
                INTO @DATA(lv_po_no).
                IF sy-subrc IS NOT INITIAL.
                  CLEAR: lv_po_no.
                ENDIF.

              ENDIF.
            ENDIF.
          CATCH /bobf/cx_frw_contrct_violation.
        ENDTRY.

        TRY.
*--Shipment Date
            lo_srv_mgr->retrieve_by_association(
              EXPORTING
                iv_node_key    = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root
                it_key         = lt_key
                iv_association = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_association-root-stop
                iv_fill_data   = abap_true
                iv_edit_mode   = /bobf/if_conf_c=>sc_edit_read_only
              IMPORTING
                et_data        = lt_stop_data
                eo_message     = lo_message ).

            IF lt_stop_data IS NOT INITIAL.
              lt_stop_data_temp = CORRESPONDING #( lt_stop_data ).
              SORT lt_stop_data_temp BY stop_seq_pos.
              READ TABLE lt_stop_data_temp WITH KEY stop_seq_pos = lc_l
              ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_stop_data>) BINARY SEARCH.
              IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
                DATA(lv_ship_timestamp) = <lfs_stop_data>-plan_trans_time.
              ENDIF.
              IF lv_ship_timestamp IS NOT INITIAL.
                lv_temp_date = lv_ship_timestamp.
                lv_ship_date = lv_temp_date+4(2) && |/| && lv_temp_date+6(2) && |/| && lv_temp_date+0(4).
              ENDIF.
            ENDIF.
          CATCH /bobf/cx_frw_contrct_violation.
        ENDTRY.

*--Ship to Address
        IF lv_consignee_id IS NOT INITIAL.
          SELECT addressid
          FROM zi_us_tm_apo_location
          WHERE location  = @lv_consignee_id
          ORDER BY PRIMARY KEY
          INTO @DATA(lv_adrs_no) UP TO 1 ROWS.
          ENDSELECT.
          IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
            SELECT SINGLE addressid,cityname,postalcode,streetname,housenumber,country,region,addresseename1
            FROM i_addrorgnamepostaladdress WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
            WHERE addressid = @lv_adrs_no
            INTO @DATA(ls_shipto_addrs).
            IF sy-subrc IS NOT INITIAL.
              CLEAR: ls_shipto_addrs.
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

        TRY.
*--Item Details
            lo_srv_mgr->retrieve_by_association(
              EXPORTING
                iv_node_key    = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root
                it_key         = lt_key
                iv_association = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_association-root-item_tr
                iv_fill_data   = abap_true
                iv_edit_mode   = /bobf/if_conf_c=>sc_edit_read_only
              IMPORTING
                et_data        = lt_tor_item
                eo_message     = lo_message ).

            IF lt_tor_item IS NOT INITIAL.
              lt_tor_item_temp = CORRESPONDING #( lt_tor_item ).
              SORT lt_tor_item_temp BY item_cat main_cargo_item.

              IF lt_tor_item_temp IS NOT INITIAL.
                READ TABLE lt_tor_item_temp WITH KEY item_cat = lc_prd main_cargo_item = abap_true
                ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_tor_item_temp>) BINARY SEARCH.
                IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
                  DATA(lv_plant) = <lfs_tor_item_temp>-loading_point_id.
                ENDIF.
              ENDIF.
            ENDIF.
          CATCH /bobf/cx_frw_contrct_violation.
        ENDTRY.

        TRY.
*--Sold to Party
            lo_srv_mgr->retrieve_by_association(
              EXPORTING
                iv_node_key    = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root
                it_key         = lt_key
                iv_association = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_association-root-party
                iv_fill_data   = abap_true
                iv_edit_mode   = /bobf/if_conf_c=>sc_edit_read_only
              IMPORTING
                et_data        = lt_soldtoparty
                eo_message     = lo_message ).

            IF lt_soldtoparty IS NOT INITIAL.
              lt_soldtoparty_temp = CORRESPONDING #( lt_soldtoparty ).
              SORT lt_soldtoparty_temp BY party_rco.
              READ TABLE lt_soldtoparty_temp WITH KEY party_rco = lc_sp
              ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_soldtoparty>) BINARY SEARCH.
              IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
                DATA(lv_soldtoparty) = <lfs_soldtoparty>-party_id.
              ENDIF.
            ENDIF.
          CATCH /bobf/cx_frw_contrct_violation.
        ENDTRY.

*--Customer Email Address
        IF lv_soldtoparty IS NOT INITIAL.
          SELECT b~emailaddress
          FROM i_buspartaddress WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS AS a
          INNER JOIN i_addressemailaddress_2 WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS AS b
          ON b~addressid = a~addressid
          WHERE a~businesspartner = @lv_soldtoparty
          INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_customer_email).
        ENDIF.

*--Account Detail
        SELECT SINGLE b~addresseename1 FROM i_buspartaddress WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS AS a
        INNER JOIN i_addrorgnamepostaladdress AS b ON b~addressid = a~addressid
        WHERE a~businesspartner = @lv_consignee_id
        INTO @DATA(lv_account).


        IF lv_purch_org = lc_comp1.  "'50000207'.
*--company1 Logo from se78.
          CALL METHOD cl_ssf_xsf_utilities=>get_bds_graphic_as_bmp
            EXPORTING
              p_object       = lc_graphics
              p_name         = lc_company1
              p_id           = lc_bmap
              p_btype        = lc_bcol
            RECEIVING
              p_bmp          = lv_hama_logo
            EXCEPTIONS
              not_found      = 1
              internal_error = 2
              OTHERS         = 3.
          IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
            DATA(lv_logo_xstring) = lv_hama_logo.
          ENDIF.

        ELSEIF lv_purch_org = lc_comp2.  "'50000201'.
*--company2 Logo from se78.
          CALL METHOD cl_ssf_xsf_utilities=>get_bds_graphic_as_bmp
            EXPORTING
              p_object       = lc_graphics
              p_name         = lc_company2
              p_id           = lc_bmap
              p_btype        = lc_bcol
            RECEIVING
              p_bmp          = lv_ener_logo
            EXCEPTIONS
              not_found      = 1
              internal_error = 2
              OTHERS         = 3.
          IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
            lv_logo_xstring = lv_ener_logo.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

*--Fetch Sender's Email Id.
        APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lr_name ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_name>).
        <lfs_name>-sign   = cl_abap_range=>sign-including.
        <lfs_name>-option = cl_abap_range=>option-equal.
        <lfs_name>-low    = lc_name1.

        APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lr_name ASSIGNING <lfs_name>.
        <lfs_name>-sign   = cl_abap_range=>sign-including.
        <lfs_name>-option = cl_abap_range=>option-equal.
        <lfs_name>-low    = lc_name2.

        SELECT changeid,name,low FROM zi_tvarvctableforcusto
        WHERE changeid = @lc_us_tm004
        AND name IN @lr_name ORDER BY PRIMARY KEY
        INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_tvarvc).

        IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
          IF lv_purch_org = lc_comp1.
            READ TABLE lt_tvarvc WITH KEY name = lc_name1 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_tvarvc>) BINARY SEARCH.
            IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
              DATA(lv_sender_email) = <lfs_tvarvc>-low.
            ENDIF.
          ELSEIF lv_purch_org = lc_comp2.
            READ TABLE lt_tvarvc WITH KEY name = lc_name2 ASSIGNING <lfs_tvarvc> BINARY SEARCH.
            IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
              lv_sender_email = <lfs_tvarvc>-low.
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

        IF lv_sender_email IS INITIAL.
          lv_msg = |Sender Email not maintained in Table: ZI_TVARVCTABLEFORCUSTO - Return|.
          log_free_text if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error lv_msg.
          rp_status = 2.
          save_log_and_return.
        ENDIF.

**************************************************************************
*--EMAIL SENDING LOGIC
**************************************************************************
        IF lt_customer_email IS NOT INITIAL.
          TRY.
              DATA(lo_mail) = cl_bcs_mail_message=>create_instance( ).

              lo_mail->set_sender( CONV #( lv_sender_email ) ).

              LOOP AT lt_customer_email ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_cust_emails>).
                lo_mail->add_recipient( CONV #( <lfs_cust_emails>-emailaddress ) ).
              ENDLOOP.

              lo_mail->set_subject( TEXT-001 ).

*--Add Logo to Body of Email.
              lv_company_logo = 'Company Logo'(002).
              lo_mail->add_attachment( cl_bcs_mail_binarypart=>create_instance(
                                         iv_content      = lv_logo_xstring
                                         iv_content_type = lc_type      "'image/bmp'
                                         iv_filename     = lv_company_logo ) ).

              lv_html =
                |<html>| &&
                 |<body style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif;">| &&
                  |<div style="text-align:left;">| &&
                  |<img src="cid:{ lv_company_logo }" width="1000" height="150" alt="Company Logo" /> <br>| &&
                  |</div>| &&
                |<hr/>| &&
                 |<p><b>Your company1 order has been shipped - PO# { lv_po_no } on { lv_ship_date }.</b></p>| &&
                 |<p>Thank you for your recent order. The items on your order have been shipped. See below for details.</p><br> | &&

                |<table cellpadding="5" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 80%;">| &&
                 |<tr><th style="border: none; text-align: left;"><b>Order Details</b></th>| &&
                 |<th style="border: none; text-align: left;"><b>Shipping Address</b></th></tr>| &&

*--Tags for Order Details & Shipping Address
                |<tr><td style="border: none;">Order no: { |{ lv_sales_order ALPHA = OUT }| }</td>| &&
                |<td style="border: none;">{ ls_shipto_addrs-addresseename1 }</td></tr>| &&

                |<tr><td style="border: none;">Shipping Method: { ls_delv_det-shippingcondition } - { ls_delv_det-shippingconditionname }</td>| &&
                |<td style="border: none;">{ ls_shipto_addrs-housenumber },{ ls_shipto_addrs-streetname }</td></tr>| &&

                |<tr><td style="border: none;">Ship Date: { lv_ship_date }</td>| &&
                |<td style="border: none;">{ ls_shipto_addrs-cityname },{ ls_shipto_addrs-region },| &&
                |{ ls_shipto_addrs-postalcode }</td></tr>| &&

                |<tr><td style="border: none;">Tracking no: { lv_tracking_no }</td>| &&
                |<td style="border: none;">{ ls_shipto_addrs-country }</td></tr></table><br>| &&

*--Dark Lines
             |<hr style="margin-bottom: 0.6cm; border: 1px solid black;"/>| &&
             |<hr style="border: 1px solid black;"/>| &&
*--Populate Item Details
                |<table cellpadding="5" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 80%;">| &&
                  |<tr>| &&
                    |<th style="border: none; text-align: left;"><b>Item Number</b></th>| &&
                    |<th style="border: none; text-align: left;"><b>Material Number</b></th>| &&
                    |<th style="border: none; text-align: left;"><b>Description</b></th>| &&
                    |<th style="border: none; text-align: left;"><b>Order Quantity</b></th>| &&
                  |</tr>|.

              LOOP AT lt_tor_item ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_item>).
                IF <lfs_item>-item_cat = lc_prd AND <lfs_item>-main_cargo_item = abap_true.
                  lv_qty = <lfs_item>-qua_pcs_val.
                  lv_html_item = lv_html_item &&
                      |<tr>| &&
                         |<td style="border: none;">{ |{ <lfs_item>-item_id ALPHA = OUT }| }</td>| &&
                         |<td style="border: none;">{ |{ <lfs_item>-product_id ALPHA = OUT }| }</td>| &&
                         |<td style="border: none;">{ <lfs_item>-item_descr }</td>| &&
                         |<td style="border: none;">{ lv_qty }</td>| &&
                       |</tr>|.
                  CLEAR: lv_qty.
                ENDIF.
              ENDLOOP.

              IF lv_html_item IS NOT INITIAL.
                lv_html = lv_html && lv_html_item.
              ENDIF.

*--Dark Lines
              lv_html = lv_html && |</table>| && |<hr style="margin-bottom: 0.6cm; border: 1px solid black;"/>| &&
              |<hr style="border: 1px solid black;"/>| &&

              |<b>Tracking URL</b><br>| &&
              |<a href="{ lv_tracking_url }" style="color: #87CEEB; text-decoration: underline;" target="_blank">{ lv_tracking_url }</a>| &&
              |<br/>| &&
              |</body></html>|.


              LOOP AT lt_atf_file ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_atf_file>).
                IF <lfs_atf_file>-content IS NOT INITIAL.
                  lo_mail->add_attachment(
                    cl_bcs_mail_binarypart=>create_instance(
                      iv_content      = <lfs_atf_file>-content
                      iv_content_type = lc_mimetype               "'application/pdf'
                      iv_filename     = CONV #( <lfs_atf_file>-file_name )
                    )
                  ).
                ENDIF.
              ENDLOOP.

              lo_mail->set_main( cl_bcs_mail_textpart=>create_text_html( lv_html ) ).

              lo_mail->send( IMPORTING ev_mail_status = DATA(lv_status) ).

              IF lv_status = 'E'.
                lv_msg = |Email Sending Failed - Return|.
                log_free_text if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error lv_msg.
                rp_status = 2.
                save_log_and_return.
              ELSE.
                lv_msg = |Email Sent Successfully|.
                log_free_text if_bali_constants=>c_severity_status lv_msg.
                rp_status = 1.
                save_log_and_return.
              ENDIF.

            CATCH cx_bcs_mail INTO DATA(lx_mail).
          ENDTRY.
        ELSE.
          lv_msg = |Customer Email not maintained in Customer Master - Return|.
          log_free_text if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error lv_msg.
          rp_status = 2.
          save_log_and_return.
        ENDIF.

      WHEN lc_hazard_form.  "ZTM_TOR_HAZARD_FORM.
        rp_status = 1.
      WHEN OTHERS.
    ENDCASE.

  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Rethinking Talent, Learning, and AI: Insights from Anil Kumar
entry.content:
In a rapidly evolving business landscape shaped by artificial intelligence, the traditional rules of learning and talent development are being rewritten. In a recent podcast conversation with Anil Kumar, Professor of Business Analytics at Texas A&M University Commerce, a compelling narrative emerged—one that challenges organizations, educators, and professionals to rethink how they approach skills, hiring, and innovation in the age of generative AI. 🚀

AI in the Classroom: The Two Camps 🤖

Generative AI is already deeply embedded in the academic experience. According to Anil Kumar, students today fall into two distinct camps—those who leverage AI to accelerate learning and those who risk over-reliance, potentially weakening critical thinking. This “spectrum of use” highlights a broader truth: AI’s value depends entirely on how it is applied.

Interestingly, today’s students are “AI natives,” seamlessly integrating tools like ChatGPT or Google’s NotebookLM into their daily workflows. Unlike previous generations who had to adapt to digital tools, these students treat AI as an extension of their thinking process. This shift presents both an opportunity and a challenge for educators and employers alike. 🎓

From Usage to Mastery: Teaching Critical Thinking with AI 🧠

Rather than resisting AI, forward-thinking educators are designing curricula that embrace it. In Anil Kumar’s classroom, AI use is not optional—it’s required. However, the emphasis is not on output but on judgment. Students must submit AI-generated responses alongside their own analysis, demonstrating how they refine, critique, and improve upon machine outputs.

This approach flips the narrative. Instead of AI replacing thinking, it becomes a catalyst for deeper reasoning. The goal is to “reverse engineer critical thinking,” ensuring students remain active participants in the learning process rather than passive consumers. 💡

The Skills That Matter Now (and Always) 🔑

Despite the technological shift, some fundamentals remain unchanged. Problem-solving, creativity, curiosity, communication, and critical thinking continue to be indispensable. However, one new competency stands out: AI fluency.

AI fluency doesn’t mean technical mastery—it means understanding the landscape, knowing when and how to use AI tools, and recognizing their limitations. As Anil Kumar emphasizes, professionals don’t need to be experts, but they must be conversant. In a world where AI is ubiquitous, ignorance is no longer an option. 📊

A Broken Hiring Model? Time for Reinvention 🏗️

One of the most provocative insights from the discussion centers on hiring practices. Today’s recruitment process often involves AI-generated job descriptions being evaluated against AI-optimized resumes—a system where “bots are evaluating bots.” The human element, ironically, is diminished.

This raises a critical question: Are organizations overlooking true potential?

Anil Kumar suggests a paradigm shift—moving away from static resumes toward dynamic “artifacts.” These could be digital portfolios, projects, or real-world solutions that showcase a candidate’s journey and capabilities over time. Such artifacts provide a richer, more authentic view of talent than traditional credentials ever could. 📁

The Rise of the Builder Mindset 🛠️

A recurring theme is the power of creation. Whether it’s a high school student building a housing platform or an English major training engineers in AI prompt design, success increasingly belongs to those who can identify problems and build solutions—regardless of formal training.

This “builder mindset” thrives on curiosity and observation. It challenges the notion that only elite institutions produce innovation. Instead, it democratizes creativity, empowering individuals to leverage accessible tools and make meaningful contributions. 🌍

Bridging the Gap: What Organizations Can Do 🤝

For companies—particularly those navigating procurement, technology, or sourcing—the implications are significant. Traditional engagement models with universities (career fairs, internships) may no longer suffice.

Anil Kumar առաջարկs a more continuous, mentorship-driven approach. Imagine professionals mentoring students throughout their academic journey, reviewing their projects, and offering feedback. This not only builds stronger talent pipelines but also ensures alignment between academic preparation and industry needs.

Such a shift requires minimal time investment but delivers high impact—creating a feedback loop that benefits both students and organizations. 📈

The Bigger Picture: Opportunity vs. Inaction 🌟

The overarching message is clear: opportunities in AI and business transformation are vast, but they are not guaranteed. Organizations must actively seek, recognize, and act on them. History has shown that ignoring emerging trends—like Blockbuster did with Netflix—can have lasting consequences.

The future of business is not just about digital transformation; it’s about talent transformation. Those who embrace change, remain curious, and challenge conventional thinking will lead the way. 🔮

LISTEN TO THE PODCAST HERE:

APPLE:  https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/episode-033-how-ai-in-the-classroom-is-shaping-the/id1764078646?i=1000774843043

 SPOTIFY:  https://open.spotify.com/episode/1quMtK54iobVbiFc3nFeHT?si=435b13836a024b15

@KAHYON01 - these are helpful blogs.  If you are looking for information not in a standard SAP CDS view, you would have to explore populating a custom field with a BADI  such as Modification of Header Custom Fields in Sales Documents (SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD) or Manufacturing Order Component - Update on Processing/Save ( BD_COBADICFL_MFGORDER_CMP) and extending an appropriate CDS view.

 

Here are some links on extending these forms you mention.  Please provide points if helpful

 

PP More Data List.  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/219418e4f5ff4501a36e36c00296aa65.html?q=PP_MOR_DATA_LIST

Ultimately, if the published BADIs don't suffice, nor the CDS, you can put in a customer influence request.  If that won't work, build an output (integrated with EventMesh) in BTP or otherwise

entry.title: Importing users failed. Error is: Missing required displayName attribute
entry.content:
Dear Sap 

we discovered we are unable to impot users from csv files in Sap ID service

Since one week at least  we noted the import of these csv files exported from Sap IBP  fails  with the error:

First 0 users are imported. User import has been interrupted for users with email address "name.surname@xxxx.com". The reason for this error is as follows: "Failed to create user in IdDS: {"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"],"status":"400","scimType":"invalidValue","detail":"Missing required displayName attribute."}".

Not clear at all the reason of the error  , we always used this method since years.

We found note 3240210 , seems exacttly the issue

BUt again, we need to clarify what is changed and why , for sure nodoby of us  had set this field as Requested .

 

Regards

Roberto

entry.title: Re: How to restrict access to specific cost centers in SA...
entry.content:
@momeet_iqbald7aa85 - 

Many ways to do this, none are perfect.  Security is the first place to think about, but the honest truth is this is lots of upkeep, and other roles can expose cost centers even if restricted in one set.  Listing options - probably a combination works best 

1. If you are using the new apps, try to maintain default values for cost centers.  Will help some

2. If you are using the old app (e.g. ME21N) one option is a personal value list. 

3. Could use entry aid config to map a material group to GL account by default, and then put a substitution in FI

4. BADIs are available - would only restrict posting, wouldn't make visibility less

5. Otherwise, security restrictions are a pain to maintain, sometimes not applicable. Not worth pursuing in my opinion, as if you give them a second or third role would need to maintain cost centers there too, if even available. 

 

Likes always appreciated

entry.title: Re: Eligibility rule workaround in Non-EC instance
entry.content:
@petre_manvelishvili I would stay away from Static groups, they are too much to maintain. It is worse practice to use them.

Variable Pay is a whole different story, in variable pay it starts with employee history: customer needs to load the records for each employee. Then you need to have designed bonus plans that the records will be matched with. Only then will employees show on a worksheet. On top of that you can use some additional UDF or "Manager eligibility rules" values that will exclude someone completely (even though they have employee history records loaded). But usually we don't use those because customer has already provided records that are eligible and prorated correctly. As a side note, and as much as we like to help others on our spare time the volume of questions makes us want to remind it : if you are not certified in Variable pay, don't try implementing it please.

entry.title: SEN Cloud Producer errors of "Bad allocation" or "Winmain: operator->() called on nullptr"
entry.content:
Hi guys, anyone had issues like these with SEN Cloud Producer? I have a ticket in to SAP Support two weeks ago, but thought I would ask here too. 

"Bad allocation" error displays about 75% through the progress bar when you do Server Bulk Actions > Update All Objects from Server, or when you Synchronize Workarea. The process ends on random objects, not the same SEN object every time. 

"Winmain: operator->() called on nullptr" when you close Producer using the X button in the upper right corner OR when you do Workspace > Exit.

The only way to recover is to kill Producer with Task Manager and then relaunch from SEN Manager. 

These errors occur about 75% of the time when you use Producer on a daily basis. Some Producer sessions encounter no errors. 

I have deleted the workarea and recreated it on the PC. I have deleted Producer and reinstalled it from scratch. I have adjusted the Watchdog "heartbeat" to wait longer before erroring.  Deleting the cache folder for Producer seems to help but only for one or two sessions of Producer, then the errors come back. 

My PC has about 25% free space on a 256GB SDD. VPN speed is good, consistently above 50 Mbps up and down. 

There is nothing in me.sap.com or in the SAP Community about this. I thought I would ask here. Thanks for your input!  @ShaneLipke

entry.title: Unexpected Connections Consumption in SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently implementing a Supplier Portal using SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition.

Our current subscription includes:

1,000 Active Users
0 Connections

Authentication is performed through SAP Cloud Identity Services (Identity Authentication - IAS) as our Identity Provider (IdP).

Due to an omission during the commercial contracting process, our subscription was provisioned with Active Users only, while the Connections metric was not included. We are already discussing this situation with our SAP Account Team, but we would like to better understand both the technical behavior and whether anyone has experienced a similar situation.

During our testing, we observed that every external login generates Connections, even though our subscription does not include this metric.

From a technical perspective, we would like to know:

1. Is the generation of the Connections metric mandatory in SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition?
2. Is there any way to completely prevent the generation of the Connections metric through Role Collections, authorizations or SAP Build Work Zone configuration?

From a commercial perspective, we would also appreciate guidance from anyone who has experienced a similar situation.

Any guidance or shared experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Error when importing spreadsheet in configuration act...
entry.content:
Dear @DBoneco,

SAP Help confirms that when configuration content is imported from a configuration activity via Table View -> Import Spreadsheet -> Contents, the system launches a separate app called Upload Business Configuration in a new tab. This means export can still work correctly, while import fails if the separate upload app cannot be resolved or opened

More details below:

SAP Help – How to Upload Business Configurations

The required access should be checked at business catalog level, not only at business role name level. Even if the user has SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT / Business Process Configuration Expert the actual assigned business role should be verified, especially if it was copied, adjusted, or not updated after an upgrade

More details below:

SAP Help – Upload Business Configuration
SAP Help – Business Roles and Catalogs
SAP Help – Manage Business Role Changes After Upgrade

Recommended checks:

1. Open the Upload Business Configuration app directly from the Fiori Launchpad / App Finder
If the same error appears the issue is confirmed at the app/navigation-target level, not at configuration activity level

2. In Maintain Business Roles, check the actual business role assigned to the user and confirm that the required business catalog for business configuration/customizing upload is assigned and active

3. If the role is copied from SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT, compare it with the current SAP-delivered role template or check role changes after upgrade, because missing or outdated catalog assignments can cause this type of Fiori target resolution issue

4. If the required catalog is assigned correctly and the app still cannot be opened, raise an SAP incident. SAP KBA 3658993 specifically refers to the Upload Business Configuration app and the navigation intent #BusinessConfiguration-upload in Public Cloud

More details below:

SAP KBA 3658993 – Error when accessing Upload Business Configuration app

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Transfer of Work Instruction without DIR
entry.content: Hi, as explained above, DMS/DIR integration will not send physical files to DM in standard, only file urls, so you can load them from POD via url. For physical files, you can evaluate extending the standard integration using Integration Suite to download and process the files from the urls, and create and assign them in DM Work Instructions via APIs. Hope it clarifies! Br, Manoel

entry.title: Re: Business Data Architecture: Defining Business Meaning
entry.content:
Hi @SubbuDX 
 
Conceptually, a metric defines what the business measures (a business outcome), while measures define how it is analyzed and dimensions provide context.
From an implementation perspective, it is valid to model “Metric” as a dimension in a Metric Data Product (more on this in a later post). This anchors the measures native to the metric (e.g. Actual, Budget, Estimate), and allows multiple measures to be handled consistently within a single fact view.
 
If we consider first-order metrics as those defined directly on source or harmonized data, then second-order metrics can be understood as metrics derived from other metrics (e.g. Margin based on Revenue and Cost). However, this only holds if the result represents a new business outcome. Otherwise, it remains an analytical calculation (measure) applied to a metric.
 
Third-order metrics such as variances and ratios are not metrics in this sense, but analytical measures applied to metrics. In Datasphere these will be based on native measures from a fact view, but the final calculation is done in an Analytical Model because it often needs to be calculated after aggregation.
 
Regarding ownership vs. execution:
The business defines the metric (what it means), but they should not implement it themselves. Implementation is done once by the platform owning that business domain, ensuring consistency and reuse across tools.
For measures (e.g. actuals), this is typically a collaboration:
  • the business defines how the measure should be interpreted
  • the platform/BI team implements and steers it consistently across the landscape

entry.title: Re: Issues with EBS via MBC – IBC94 Status and Garbled Co...
entry.content:
Hi SAP4PC,

Short answer: This is almost certainly a character-encoding / charset corruption introduced by the Content Modifier step in the MBC integration flow — not a genuinely missing :62F. Forcing CAMT.FIN.MT940.* into "standard MT940" with a CPI Content Modifier is the wrong lever: the connector parses MT940 byte-for-byte, and once the charset is wrong, the entire payload becomes unreadable, so RFEBKA00 fails at the end of the statement and reports :62F missing.

Have others seen this after changing the message type via MBC's Content Modifier?

Yes. The "Content Modifier" is a step in SAP Integration Suite / Cloud Integration (CPI), which is what runs inside the MBC tenant flow — so the transformation happens in the cloud flow, before the connector. SAP Integration Suite is an open and modular iPaaS supporting enterprise-wide integration needs, and the Content Modifier there rewrites headers/properties and the message body. Reference: SAP Help Portal

Constraint: The MBC connector on the S/4HANA side does not modify payloads — the connector doesn't modify the payload of the messages. So any garbling is being created upstream in the CPI Content Modifier, not in S/4HANA. Reference: SAP Help

Is it encoding / character-set handling?

Yes — this is the most likely root cause. In CPI, if the Content Modifier (or the downstream HTTP adapter) doesn't carry the correct charset, the payload gets re-encoded against a wrong/default code page and non-ASCII bytes are mangled. The HTTP receiver adapter needs a byte array and uses the defined charset for encoding (or ASCII if nothing is defined); characters that don't exist in the used encoding are replaced by "?". The usual fix is to set the charset explicitly (property CamelCharsetName) in the Content Modifier. SAP documents this as a known class of problem: see Avoiding Encoding Issues (https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/avoiding-encoding-issues) and Character Encodings – Background (https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/character-encodings-background-information). SAP Blogs

This matches the MT940 world directly: MT940 statements delivered in a non-Unicode code page (e.g. Windows-1256, GB2312, Shift-JIS) render as garbage when the consuming side assumes a different encoding.

Why is :62F reported missing — and why "closing first" rather than :60F?

It isn't checking closing first. :62F missing (message FV151) is a statement-completion check that fires at the end of parsing. The parser reads sequentially; if it never recognizes a valid closing-balance record before the statement terminates, it raises FV151.

 

The SSF warning ("No security features active … for Message Type MT940")

Secondary — likely a symptom of the rename, not the cause of corruption. SSF security in the connector is maintained per message type (and is optional). If you don't specify a Message Type, the settings apply to all; for specific message types you can override the defaults. By renaming the type to MT940, the connector now looks up an SSF profile for MT940 and finds none, hence the warning. It tells you the message-type-keyed configuration no longer matches — verify it isn't also bypassing tamper protection you expect. Device Report

What I would actually do

The connector can process several formats directly via a selection variant — for MT940, MX camt.053, MT942, or MX camt.052 the report RFEBKA00 is executed with the variant configured in Maintain Selection Variants for Bank Account. So you don't need a Content Modifier to "make it MT940"; you need the right format, correctly encoded, with a matching variant. Reference: SAP help and SAP Community

Constraints to verify (in order):

  • Encoding in the flow: In the MBC/CPI flow, confirm the Content Modifier sets CamelCharsetName (and the Content-Type charset) to what the bank actually sends; compare the raw payload before vs. after the modifier in Notepad++/hex to see exactly where the bytes break.
  • Whether the transformation is needed at all: The original CAMT.FIN.MT940.* was already producing a clean payload (your :60F/:62F were intact — only the type recognition was off). Re-classifying/registering that message type + selection variant is cleaner than re-formatting the body. Consider asking MBC to deliver ISO CAMT.053 instead, which avoids MT character-set fragility entirely.
  • Message-type registration: After any rename, set the matching Selection Variant and the SSF profile entry for that type, or use the generic (blank message-type) SSF entry.
  • Connector monitor: Use t/BSNAGT/MONITOR o inspect the inbound payload as received, to confirm the garbling is already present on arrival (i.e. created upstream in CPI).

Summary: Don't fight the symptom (:62F / FV151). Revert or fix the CPI Content Modifier so it preserves the bank's encoding (set the charset explicitly), or try to ask MBC to deliver a clean, supported format (CAMT.053, or correctly-encoded MT940) that you register with a Selection Variant and matching SSF entry. The missing :62F and the garbled file are the same problem: an unreadable, wrongly-encoded payload.

Hope it helps.

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: BTE 1820 custom code is not triggering during payment...
entry.content: If FIBF shows BTE 1820 as active but your code never gets hit, First verify that the correct product and process module are actually assigned to event 1820 and that the function module is activated. Also make sure your payment scenario really reaches that BTE, as some payment methods or configurations can bypass specific events.

entry.title: Re: Building Your Simple Business Roles with the IAM Apps
entry.content:
Hi @the_adarsh ,

Sorry for the late response ...

To answer your question, we have to keep the history in mind. When the Public Cloud was developed, we only had the business catalog to business role concept; this works well for the purpose of persona based authorization assignment, especially in a small business when one person wearing multiple hats. However, as the product evolves, especially the user base evolves, we heard more and more dynamic usage of the product. Customers want to have precise control and authorizations than a blanket covering all. This gave the birth of IAM Apps approach.

However, as of today, not all the Fiori Apps have IAM Apps associated with them. This situation will improve over the time.

Now back to your question: if the Fiori apps you are working with have the IAM Apps Variants assigned, then go ahead taking the IAM Apps approach. It is easy and precise. Otherwise, you still need to use the business catalogs for the time being, even it might not be perfect for your situation.

Hope this explanation helps.

Regards,

George

entry.title: Purchase order pricing: ZCOS manual condition proposed with last historical value
entry.content:
When creating a purchase order in ME21N, custom condition type ZCOS is proposed automatically in the item Conditions tab, although ZCOS is expected to be a manual/suggested value that must be reviewed and maintained by the buyer per item.

Original business case: PO was created with different ZCOS values by item: item 10 = 37,000, item 20 = 33,000 and item 30 = 35,000. When a new PO was created for the same vendor/material context, the system proposed the last ZCOS value used in the previous PO, i.e. 35,000 from PO item 30.

Additional reproduction evidence in ME21N shows the same behavior pattern: after entering ZNAC Standard PO data, supplier , purchasing org , purchasing group  , company code , material XXX, quantity 20 KG and plant , the Conditions tab is populated with ZCOS (Freight/Quantity) as 5 MXN, with a condition value of 1 USD.

Business expectation: ZCOS is not an automatic tariff/rate and should not be calculated or updated automatically. It should be entered or explicitly confirmed manually per PO item based on the specific commercial conditions applicable to that purchase.

We need SAP Support to confirm whether th
